lede: A look at May 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ellen White (footballer) and Zita of Bourbon-Parma, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Ghana
events.desc: In Ghana, 129 football fans die in what became known as the Accra Sports Stadium disaster. The deaths are caused by a stampede (caused by the firing of tear gas by police personnel at the stadium) that followed a controversial decision by the referee.

events.title: Armenia
events.desc: Armenian forces capture Shusha, marking a major turning point in the First Nagorno-Karabakh War.

events.title: Florida
events.desc: In Florida, United States, Liberian freighter MV Summit Venture collides with the Sunshine Skyway Bridge over Tampa Bay, making a 430-meter (1,400 ft) section of the southbound span collapse. Thirty-five people in six cars and a Greyhound bus fall 46 metres (150 ft) into the water and die.

events.title: Iranian Jews
events.desc: Iranian Jewish businessman Habib Elghanian is executed by firing squad in Tehran, prompting the mass exodus of the once 100,000-strong Jewish community of Iran.

events.title: Food and Drug Administration
events.desc: The Food and Drug Administration announces it will approve birth control as an additional indication for Searle's Enovid, making Enovid the world's first approved oral contraceptive pill.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Channel Islands are liberated from Nazi occupation.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Second Battle of Artois between German and French forces.

events.title: Second Schleswig War
events.desc: Second Schleswig War: The Danish navy defeats the Austrian and Prussian fleets in the Battle of Heligoland.

events.title: Exhibition of 1761
events.desc: Exhibition of 1761, the inaugural exhibition of the Society of Artists of Great Britain, opens at Spring Gardens in London.

events.title: England
events.desc: England and Portugal formally ratify their alliance with the signing of the Treaty of Windsor, making it the oldest diplomatic alliance in the world which is still in force.

births.name: Zita of Bourbon-Parma
births.line: last Empress of the Austro-Hungarian Empire

births.name: Adam Opel
births.line: German engineer, founded the Opel Company

births.name: Manfred Eigen
births.line: German chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Marco Ferreri, Italian actor, director, and screenwriter
Ulrike Meinhof, German militant, co-founded the Red Army Faction
Leopold Figl, Austrian engineer and politician, 18th Chancellor of Austria
Friedrich Schiller, German poet, playwright, and historian

observances.text: Christian feast day: Gregory of Nazianzen (The Episcopal Church (US) and traditional Roman Catholic calendar)
